name: Vitamin D
meta_description: Learn about Vitamin D: benefits, food sources, recommended intake, and deficiency symptoms.
description: Vitamin D is a fat-soluble vitamin that plays a crucial role in bone health, immune function, and overall well-being.
recommended_intake: Adults: 600-800 IU daily (varies by age and health status)
toxicity: Excessive Vitamin D can cause hypercalcemia, kidney stones, and other health issues. Upper limit is generally 4000 IU/day for adults.
interactions: May interact with calcium supplements, certain medications, and medical conditions like kidney disease.

name: Protein
meta_description: Learn about protein: benefits, sources, and recommended intake for optimal health and fitness.
description: Protein is a macronutrient essential for building and repairing tissues, muscle growth, and various bodily functions.
recommended_intake: Adults: 0.8 grams per kilogram of body weight daily (higher for athletes and older adults)
toxicity: Excessive protein intake can strain kidneys and may contribute to dehydration. Generally well-tolerated in healthy individuals.
interactions: May interact with kidney disease, liver disease, and certain medications.

name: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
meta_description: Learn about Omega-3 fatty acids: benefits, food sources, and their role in heart and brain health.
description: Omega-3 fatty acids are polyunsaturated fats that are essential for heart health, brain function, and reducing inflammation.
recommended_intake: Adults: 250-500 mg of combined EPA and DHA daily
toxicity: High doses may increase bleeding risk, especially for those taking blood thinners. Consult a healthcare provider before taking supplements.
interactions: May interact with blood-thinning medications, diabetes medications, and hypertension medications.
